title: Odontocline laciniata
intro: Odontocline laciniata, also known as Cut-leaved Odontocline, is a species of flowering plant in the family Apiaceae, commonly found in mountainous regions. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects, making it beneficial for treating various health conditions such as wounds, fever, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Odontocline laciniata include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat colds, coughs, and other respiratory problems.
taxonomy: Odontocline laciniata is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Lamiaceae. It is a member of the tribe Lamieae. This species is native to the mountainous regions of China. The plant's classification is closely related to the genera of the Lamiaceae family, which includes plants such as mint, basil, and oregano. Further research may be needed to determine the exact classification within the tribe.
